# Competitive Landscape — Predictive History in context

**Snapshot date:** 2026-03-24
**Purpose:** Operator research context for the book and influence-tracking lane. Not a published ranking; metrics are approximate and will drift.

---

## Comparison table

Sorted by subscriber count. Predictive History highlighted.

| Rank | Channel | Subscribers | Total Views | Avg Views/Video | Created | ~Days Active | Avg New Subs/Day | Thematic Notes |
|-----:|---------|------------:|------------:|----------------:|---------|-------------:|-----------------:|----------------|
| 1 | Kings and Generals | 4.11M | 1.02B | ~507K | May 2016 | ~3,610 | ~1,139 | Animated military history and empires |
| **—** | **Predictive History (Prof. Jiang)** | **2.11M** | **~64.4M** | **~480K** | **June 20, 2023** | **~1,008** | **~2,100** | **Long-form geo-strategy, civilization, game theory, predictive lectures** |
| 2 | CaspianReport | 1.83M | 269M | ~487K | July 2010 | ~5,700 | ~321 | Deep modern geopolitics |
| 3 | Fall of Civilizations | 1.46M | ~300–400M (est.) | ~7–9M | Jan 2019 | ~2,620 | ~557 | Long-form civilizational rise and collapse |
| 4 | Zeihan on Geopolitics | 949K | ~150–200M (est.) | ~150–200K | ~Nov 2022 | ~1,220 | ~778 | Predictive geopolitics and demographics |
| 5 | HistoryLegends | 771K | 195M | ~402K | Feb 2019 | ~2,590 | ~298 | Military history, current conflicts |
| 6 | Judge Napolitano – Judging Freedom | 718–719K | 314M | ~63K | Oct 2021 | ~1,623 | ~442 | Legal and geopolitical interviews |
| 7 | The Duran | 519K | 244M | ~69K | May 2016 | ~3,600 | ~144 | Geopolitical commentary (Russia/West focus) |
| 8 | Premodernist | 402K | 24M | ~889K | Aug 2020 | ~2,057 | ~195 | Academic pre-modern history |
| 9 | Alexander Mercouris | 335K | 207M | ~110K | Nov 2017 | ~3,012 | ~111 | Daily geopolitical analysis |
| 10 | Geopolitical Futures | 69.4K | ~10–15M (est.) | ~20–30K | ~2015–2016 | ~3,650 | ~19 | Predictive international relations |
| 11 | Centre for Geopolitics (Cambridge) | 10.2K | <5M (est.) | <15K | ~2020 | ~2,000+ | ~5 | Academic lectures and panels |

---

## Key takeaways

- **Growth velocity:** ~2,100 new subscribers/day — nearly 2x the next-fastest channel in the niche (Kings and Generals at ~1,139/day), despite being less than 3 years old.
- **Per-video performance:** ~480K average views per video is competitive with channels that have been active 2–3x longer (CaspianReport ~487K, Kings and Generals ~507K). Only Premodernist (~889K) and Fall of Civilizations (~7–9M) exceed it on a per-video basis, both with far fewer total uploads.
- **Format signal:** Long-form lecture content outperforms short-form daily commentary (The Duran ~69K avg, Mercouris ~110K avg) and institutional academic panels (Cambridge ~<15K avg) by an order of magnitude per video. The audience wants depth and thesis, not frequency or credentials.
- **Institutional gap:** The 200x subscriber gap between Predictive History (2.11M) and the Cambridge Centre for Geopolitics (10.2K) is itself a data point about what audiences want versus what institutions provide — directly relevant to Jiang's own meritocracy critique (Secret History #7).
- **Recent momentum:** ~820K subscribers and ~23M views gained in the last 30 days alone (as of 2026-03-24).
